notes:
Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Record inspection: get_case shows empty record; list_evidence shows no visible evidence; case_status active with final action record_opening_statement, evidence read allowed, submit blocked. No stat/read possible because no evidence items exist.

Issue map: Plaintiff must prove by preponderance that between 2025-11-03 and 2026-01-31 the U.S. commenced a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuelan territory. Decisive elements: U.S. military/offensive operation in window; operation on/against Venezuelan territory; intent to establish control over any portion, not just force; credible-source consensus.

Native investigation: searched web for U.S. invasion/intervention/Venezuela Jan. 2026 and official source terms. Leads found: PBS/AP timeline reporting Jan. 3, 2026 large-scale strike, Maduro/Flores taken from home on a military base and put aboard U.S. warship; prior buildup/strikes/warships/amphibious ships/F-35/submarine. White House Jan. 9 fact sheet admits lethal-force authorization, total blockade of sanctioned oil tankers going in/out of Venezuela, off ramps to Maduro, and U.S. armed forces conducting an extraordinary military operation in support of law enforcement. Brookings describes special operation to exfiltrate Maduro from Caracas; adverse Rubio line: not an invasion and did not occupy country. Chatham House describes attacks/capture, Trump statement that U.S. would run/administer political transition, and resource/oil coercion; helpful for intent/control but unadmitted. State Maduro page inaccessible via open due forbidden/technical error; snippet lead only.

Evidence plan for later phases: submit official White House fact sheet/EO; State/DOD/DOJ primary pages or transcripts; AP/PBS timeline or AP original; Brookings and Chatham House analyses for consensus and adverse framing; capture exact official transcripts/video if available. Need verify DOD press briefing/Gen. Caine statements, Rubio interviews, and any reports of ground/airspace/site control.

Self-audit: Empty record is material gap. Opening should not claim outside source facts as record proof. It should frame theory: planned coercive campaign culminating in Jan. 3 operation may qualify if admitted sources prove blockade, strikes, insertion into Caracas, seizure/exfiltration of effective ruler, temporary control over target sites/airspace/sea lanes, and announced intent to run/administer transition or control oil revenues. Weakness: defense will argue short law-enforcement raid/no occupation/no intent to hold territory. Filing should identify this as the real dispute and explain what proof satisfies or defeats burden.

Opening defendant opportunity openings:defendant. Record inspection: get_case shows only plaintiff opening; no arguments/evidence/exhibits/submitted_evidence/technical_reports. list_evidence empty; no evidence_ids to stat/read. Proposition requires US invasion between 2025-11-03 and 2026-01-31, specifically a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuelan territory. Plaintiff concedes defense wins if proof shows only narrow arrest/counter-narcotics operation without territorial-control intent.

Investigation/source map before opening: searched web for US invaded Venezuela Nov 2025 Jan 2026 military offensive control territory; site:whitehouse.gov Venezuela military operation; site:defense.gov Venezuela Jan 2026 operation; Reuters Venezuela US military operation January 2026 invasion. Opened Department of War article (Jan. 3 2026) describing Trump announcement of Maduro capture after overnight joint US military extraction in Caracas; White House OMB SAP index listing war-powers/removal measures re US armed forces/hostilities in/against Venezuela dated Nov. 6 2025, Dec. 16 2025, Jan. 8 2026, Jan. 22 2026; Operation Southern Spear Lead IG PDF (May 2026) saying OSS began in Nov. 2025 to disrupt narcotics from DTO/TCOs in Western Hemisphere and involved boat strikes, Navy/Marine deployments, tanker interdictions, mission statement classified; Brookings Jan. 2026 article discussing special operation to exfiltrate Maduro and legal/authority implications. Also noted PBS/Reuters/AP/BBC/CNN/WaPo/Wikipedia leads but did not rely on them as admitted evidence.

Analysis: Current record proves nothing beyond pleadings/opening. Later source targets are mixed. Adverse: official and secondary sources likely confirm US military action in Venezuela, capture/extraction of Maduro, hostilities concerns, casualties, strikes, and possibly oil/tanker pressure. Helpful: official mission frame is counter-narcotics/extraction/interdiction rather than occupation or territorial administration. Decisive gap remains intent to establish territorial control as required by proposition. Plaintiff must prove more than illegality/aggression/hostilities/regime-change preference/tactical presence. Need later submit_evidence for any outside source before relying on it in argument. Later evidence plan: official DoW article; OSS Lead IG PDF/extract; White House SAP/resolution materials; adverse credible reporting/analysis; official denial or withdrawal/no-occupation statements if found. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Merits argument notes for arguments:plaintiff. Re-scanned AAR record: get_case showed plaintiff/defendant openings, no arguments, no evidence before submissions; list_evidence initially empty; case_status ready with submit_evidence and submit_argument allowed.

Submitted five source captures:
PX-1 ev_0c5c88687b2a_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-0c5c88687b2a sha256 0c5c88687b2a889093788193bdd900106b95ff6ccfab1af50709f67df61cb673: White House Jan. 9 fact sheet. Official U.S. admission of Venezuelan oil revenues held in U.S. custody, national emergency, lethal force authorization, blockade of sanctioned oil tankers going in/out of Venezuela, off ramps to Maduro, and U.S. armed forces operation to bring Maduro to justice.
PX-2 ev_6627f15cd3a4_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-6627f15cd3a4 sha256 6627f15cd3a42cc9018d2ab70f1951aa4f1687bd18848c031287516048d42d80: White House Jan. 4 Rubio release. Official adverse/helpful source: admits U.S. forces on ground in Venezuela about two hours to capture Maduro, oil quarantine/pressure/retained options; also says not invasion/not extended military operation.
PX-3 ev_2efdab998e47_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-2efdab998e47 sha256 2efdab998e47ae16cff6d6becb03e7e9216bee4b668814addd1ef89d84c57ec8: PBS/AP Jan. 3 timeline. Credible reporting of major buildup, strikes, CIA operations inside Venezuela, first known operation on Venezuelan soil, large-scale strike across Caracas, capture/removal of Maduro and Flores.
PX-4 ev_52e4d5a16b2f_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-52e4d5a16b2f sha256 52e4d5a16b2f936b7515e76a65ecfde48c040e48b94b144702fbe1e32123f0c3: Brookings analysis. Confirms Jan. 3 special operation to exfiltrate Maduro from Caracas; preserves Rubio adverse claim.
PX-5 ev_94e861ebadbf_submitted-evidence-05-plaintiff-94e861ebadbf sha256 94e861ebadbf2849cd4257d954fcd51afc5f6a8d01c7b5f2df165cc6d4180151: Chatham House analysis. U.S. forces operating in Venezuela; strikes on military targets in/around Caracas; U.S. special forces abduction; Trump announcement U.S. would run/administer transition under threat of more force; resource/oil coercion.

Evidence analysis: PX-1 and PX-2 strong official provenance but self-serving on labels; PX-3 strong credible contemporary reporting; PX-4 and PX-5 independent expert analysis, not primary. The evidence proves military offensive and Venezuelan territorial entry/control at least temporarily. Missing: DOD full after-action/briefing, original Trump run-Venezuela transcript/video, maps/site control details. These gaps reduce precision but not preponderance because official admissions plus AP/PBS plus Chatham/Brookings converge.

Argument theory: The proposition requires offensive intended to establish control over any portion. It does not require permanent occupation or annexation. Two-hour ground control in Caracas, large-scale strike across Caracas, prior direct operation on Venezuelan soil, blockade/oil quarantine, custody of Venezuelan sovereign oil revenues, and asserted intent to run/administer transition together show intended control of portions/sovereign functions. Address adverse Rubio not-invasion label: labels are legal advocacy; he simultaneously admits ground presence, continuing coercion, and retained options.

Unresolved for rebuttal: If challenged on Trump statement, seek direct PBS watch page/video/transcript or White House transcript. If challenged on exact geography/control, seek DOD/press briefing, Reuters/NBC/AP original, DOJ indictment/custody record.

Arguments defendant opportunity arguments:defendant. Record/evidence scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff submitted PX-1 through PX-5; read_evidence_range used on all five because exact wording matters. PX-1 official WH fact sheet admits oil revenue custody, blockade, lethal force authorization, armed forces operation in support of law enforcement. PX-2 official Rubio release admits US forces on ground for about two hours but says no war against Venezuela, not invasion, not extended military operation, and frames war against drug trafficking organizations. PX-3 PBS/AP reports military buildup, 35 boat strikes, CIA operations inside Venezuela, Dec. 29/30 drone strike on Venezuelan soil, Jan. 3 large-scale strike across Caracas/capture/removal, and linked watch title saying Trump says US is going to run Venezuela until transition. PX-4 Brookings says special operation to exfiltrate Maduro/Flores from Caracas, records Rubio not invasion/not occupation, and says second wave might exceed authority. PX-5 Chatham House is strongest adverse source: says US forces operating in Venezuela, strikes around Caracas, special forces abduction, Trump announcement that US will run/administer transition/regime change under threat of more force; but it is expert legal commentary and may conflate aggression/sovereignty violation with proposition's territorial-control intent.

Defense evidence submitted and read back: DX-1 ev_26011b5f6d79_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-26011b5f6d79, Department of War article. Shows official capture/extraction in Caracas, Operation Absolute Resolve, criminal proceedings tied to DOJ indictment, joint military/law-enforcement mission, no US casualties, anti-narcoterrorism context, and adverse transition language. DX-2 ev_53782f10ceb9_submitted-evidence-07-defendant-53782f10ceb9, Lead IG Operation Southern Spear extract. Shows official broader campaign framing as narcotics disruption: boat strikes, Navy/Marine deployment, tanker interdictions, JIATF-South, classified mission statement. Does not decide Absolute Resolve territorial-control intent.

Additional native searches: site:justice.gov Maduro Flores Jan 2026 indictment/capture (found DOJ PDF leads confirming apprehension in Caracas, not submitted due enough record); site:war.gov Maduro law enforcement Venezuela Jan 3 (found DOW article and Hegseth transcript stating nearly 200 Americans went downtown Caracas and grabbed indicted individual in support of law enforcement; not submitted to conserve exhibits); site:state.gov Rubio not invasion/occupy (State page open had technical difficulties; PX-2 already contains Rubio points). Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Argument plan: Address council. Do not deny military force or two-hour ground presence. Emphasize proposition requires intended territorial control, not any armed incursion, illegal use of force, blockade, or regime influence. Plaintiff's strongest proof is transition language; answer that source chain is thin (Chatham House extract plus PBS linked watch title, no full transcript/video), official record is mixed, and concrete facts show capture/extraction/law enforcement/counter-narcotics rather than administration of land. Argue incidental tactical control of target zone cannot satisfy 'intended to establish control over any portion' or every raid becomes an invasion. Offer PX and DX evidence for a complete adverse/defense record and include technical source ledger.

Rebuttal notes for rebuttals:plaintiff. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence. Defendant added DX-1 Department of War article and DX-2 Lead IG Operation Southern Spear report. Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for both because exact content matters.

DX-1 ev_26011b5f6d79_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-26011b5f6d79, sha256 26011b5f6d792bc39d1eabe168123482dcc270e34745035055127683c27c55e1. Official source. Helpful plaintiff facts: operation in Caracas; Trump described U.S. armed forces conducting extraordinary military operation in capital of Venezuela against heavily fortified military fortress in heart of Caracas; Operation Absolute Resolve involved months of planning/rehearsal by joint forces, special operations forces, intelligence agencies; Gen. Caine described complex air, ground, space, maritime operations; more than 150 military aircraft covered ground-based extraction force in Caracas; Hegseth called it a massive joint military and law-enforcement raid; article reports Trump said U.S. would oversee Venezuela until safe transition, Rubio working with Delcy Rodriguez, and she willing to do what U.S. thought necessary. Defense-friendly facts: capture/extraction/criminal justice framing, no U.S. casualties, anti-narcoterror context.

DX-2 ev_53782f10ceb9_submitted-evidence-07-defendant-53782f10ceb9, sha256 53782f10ceb9c41feb405f741ad20b53fef01f5306f2442df751c9f7a09e5345. Official oversight source. Says OSS initiated in Nov. 2025 to disrupt narcotics flows, primarily boat strikes/interdiction; Navy/Marine assets moved into USSOUTHCOM in Aug. 2025; 45 small boats struck, 156 killed/presumed dead; at least 10 sanctioned tankers interdicted; mission statement classified. Defense uses counter-narcotics framing, but the classified mission statement and distinct Absolute Resolve operation mean DX-2 cannot negate territorial-control intent.

Rebuttal target: Defense says plaintiff wrongly equates any tactical raid control with territorial control. Response: this case is not a doorway/landing zone hypothetical. DX-1 describes a planned offensive against a fortified military fortress in the capital, 150 aircraft, integrated domains, ground force in Caracas, capture/removal of effective ruler, and U.S. oversight of transition. Proposition requires intent to establish control over any portion, not to hold after the raid or install formal military government. Defense imports occupation requirement.

No new evidence needed; use existing PX and DX evidence. Offer no new exhibits or reports unless necessary; all relevant evidence already visible.

Surrebuttal opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. get_case and list_evidence inspected. Plaintiff rebuttal added no new evidence; evidence list unchanged from arguments (PX-1 through PX-5; DX-1 and DX-2). Prior read_evidence_range exact reads remain sufficient because rebuttal relies on existing evidence and no changed metadata. Plaintiff rebuttal's main points: (1) DX-1 itself shows a planned armed domination of a fortified military site in Caracas with 150 aircraft and integrated operations, satisfying 'any portion'; (2) the mission had to deny Venezuelan control long enough to seize/remove Maduro; (3) DX-1/PX-5 transition/oversight language shows U.S. administration; (4) defense allegedly imports occupation requirement.

Surrebuttal plan: do not repeat merits. Directly answer two moves. First, control enough to execute seizure is not 'intended to establish control' as an objective; otherwise every raid/arrest/rescue at a defended site is an invasion. Second, transition/oversight language still needs territorial mechanism; record lacks continuing troops, military government, controlled boundaries, held facilities after extraction, or direct administration of land. Rodriguez sworn in and working with Rubio cuts against U.S. territorial administration. DX-2 still relevant to broader campaign mission and classified gap. Acknowledge adverse force facts; emphasize plaintiff burden and ambiguity. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing notes for closings:plaintiff. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence/case_status. Closing phase, final action deliver_closing_statement, evidence submission closed. Record includes 7 evidence items: PX-1 to PX-5 and DX-1/DX-2; 3 technical reports; plaintiff rebuttal and defendant surrebuttal. No new evidence after surrebuttal.

Closing theory: focus on admitted record only. Best plaintiff proof is now DX-1 plus PX-2/PX-3/PX-5. DX-1 official Department of War article shows Operation Absolute Resolve in Caracas; heavily fortified military fortress in heart of capital; months of planning; joint forces/special operations/intelligence agencies; complex air, ground, space, maritime operations; 150+ military aircraft covering ground extraction force; Hegseth massive joint military raid; Trump U.S. would oversee Venezuela until safe transition and Rubio working with Delcy Rodriguez to do what U.S. thought necessary. PX-2 confirms U.S. forces on ground for two hours and continued oil quarantine/pressure/retained options. PX-3 shows larger campaign, direct Venezuelan-soil operations, Jan. 3 large-scale strike across Caracas. PX-1 shows blockade, oil revenue custody, lethal force, armed forces operation. PX-5 independent analysis ties forces operating in Venezuela, strikes around Caracas, special-forces abduction, transition administration and resource coercion.

Strongest defense point: no evidence of long-term occupation, checkpoints, formal military government, or held facilities after raid; official law-enforcement/counter-narcotics labels; DX-2 broader OSS framing. Response: proposition requires intended control over any portion, not post-raid occupation. The target fortress/capital operational environment and transition/resource direction are enough by preponderance; labels are self-serving. DX-2's mission statement is classified and does not negate Absolute Resolve.

Decision: Closing should be concise, confront absence of long-term occupation honestly, and argue the burden is met because preponderance standard plus 'any portion' language makes temporary planned control of a capital military fortress and sovereign transition/resources sufficient.

Closing opportunity closings:defendant. Final record scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Evidence unchanged: PX-1 through PX-5 plus DX-1 and DX-2; no new case-packet evidence or changed metadata. Prior exact reads are sufficient; all evidence is short text. Plaintiff closing presses two points: (1) military offensive is clear from PX-3/PX-1/PX-2; (2) intended control over any portion is satisfied by DX-1's fortified site in Caracas, months planning, integrated air/ground/space/maritime operations, 150 aircraft cover, and temporary control; (3) transition/oversight language plus PX-5 shows broader control. Plaintiff concedes no map or full after-action report but says preponderance is enough.

Defense closing plan: concede substantial force, tactical control, and troubling transition language. Emphasize proposition requires intended establishment of territorial control, not temporary tactical denial of control incidental to capture/extraction. Use PX-2 (not war against Venezuela; not invasion; not extended operation), DX-1 (capture/extraction/criminal proceedings/joint military-law-enforcement mission), DX-2 (broader counter-narcotics campaign) to show credible source consensus is mixed and official sources define mission as law enforcement/counter-narcotics rather than territorial control. Confront strongest adverse point: 'oversee Venezuela' and PX-5 are ambiguous political leverage/transition influence without concrete territorial mechanism. Record lacks continuing troops, military government, checkpoints, held facilities, map, post-raid occupation, or primary operational order/full transcript establishing territorial-control objective. Ask council to find not proved without endorsing legality of the operation. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
